mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) when FEDFUNDS is below 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 4.0 percent signals labor market tightness that triggers wage inflation expectations, prompting equity sell-offs as investors price in tighter monetary policy and margin compression.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE remains above 4.0 for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving corporate revenue forecasts upward and generating sustained positive equity returns over the quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative cumulative 60-day return in over 60% of occurrences, or the interaction term shows no incremental explanatory power over individual series in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices during a late-cycle expansion reflect capacity utilization approaching maximums, triggering short-term earnings revision optimism that pushes equity prices higher within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or flat in 70% of observations when INDPRO increases, or the signal reverses direction during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in consumer prices and the federal funds rate indicate active monetary tightening, which raises borrowing costs and compresses equity multiples, producing negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative returns when both series rise, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands the consumer base and reduces corporate labor costs per unit of output, supporting higher profit margins and generating positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months with positive payroll growth, or the series shows zero correlation with S&P 500 quarterly returns over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1.2 million units signal robust residential construction activity, which stimulates upstream supply chains and boosts corporate earnings expectations, driving positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX delivers negative 20-day returns in over 65% of triggered instances, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for interest rate movements.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between industrial production growth and unemployment declines during a post-shock recovery phase indicates inefficient capacity allocation, triggering short-term market volatility and negative weekly returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of observations when the divergence occurs, or the signal shows no predictive power outside the specified recovery regime.
